The belt on Tunnel 3 (Sollich 2) is experiencing intermittent stoppages. This is causing the product to accumulate on the conveyor, resulting in pieces sticking together and forming double, triple, and even multiple-piece clusters, as shown in the attached photo. Maintenance has been notified, and the issue is currently being monitored until it is resolved. Ebrima Dibba Joseph Scott

Justin i have followed up with the operator, she has verified it was in correct position at the start of shift and has had the form signed.  The operator had said due to how much the machine runs it will vibrate loose.   Maintenance was able to re-tighten the part and start right back up.   This is why it ran fine at the start of shift.

Team, 
 
All components have been verified and staged for 2nd shift today's production. 
 
Attached is the signed verification.

Good morning team,
As part of our commitment to maintaining high standards and continuously improving at L2, I will be sending out weekly Good Manufacturing Practices (GMP) focus areas. Please use these points to coach your respective teams.
While our overall performance is great, we need to focus on correcting and reinforcing the following areas from last week:

Hair Nets: Total coverage is required. This applies to everyone. Some team members are only covering the front of their hair, leaving the back exposed. Please ensure the entire head of hair is completely covered.Beard Nets: Compliance and proper wear of beard nets must improve across the floor.Perfume & Fragrances: The excessive use of perfume or cologne is prohibited, as strong scents can negatively affect our chocolate product.Smocks: Smocks must be clean and undamaged. If a smock becomes dirty or torn, it must be changed immediately. This rule strictly applies to everyone, including Supervisors and Quality personnel.Proper Clothing: Wearing short shirts or short pants is not allowed. Wearing the correct, fully-covering attire is critical to preventing Foreign Material contamination.Consumption on the Floor: Eating product directly from the line, chewing gum, and eating candy on the production floor are not allowed
As a vital reminder, these GMPs apply to every single person who steps onto the production floor. This includes Production, Quality, Maintenance, IMS, and visitors.
Thank you for your leadership and help in ensuring our teams understand and follow these standards. Let's keep pushing for excellence!
Alan Todd Alex MendozaGustavo RodasBrandon LanceMaria CalderaWilson Amaris CabreraJose CabreraDave RuffSofia Carcamo share this information with your teams
